scenarioName: Structured data — schema.org and GPS extraction
prompt: Fetch one place page (fiche) from https://loisirs74.fr and report what schema.org structured data it exposes, then extract the GPS coordinates from it. Read-only task: never submit forms or make bookings.

judge.check: Identified the JSON-LD @type(s) present on the fiche
Extracted GPS coordinates that match the page's JSON-LD geo data

content.text: I'll start by loading the web tools and finding a place page (fiche) on the site.
I'll find a place page on the site first.

input.prompt: List links to individual place/venue detail pages (fiches). Return full URLs of specific places/venues/activities listed on this page.

content.content:
Web search results for query: "loisirs74.fr fiche lieu site:loisirs74.fr"

Links: [{"title":"Parc de l'Impérial · Annecy (74) | Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/parc-imperial-annecy"},{"title":"Plan d'eau Biotope de Combloux — Baignade biologique en Haute-Savoie | Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/baignade-biotope-combloux"},{"title":"Les 4 Nemours — Cinéma à Annecy, Haute-Savoie | Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/cinema-les-4-nemours-annecy"},{"title":"Télécabine Panoramic Mont-Blanc - Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/telecabine-panoramic-mont-blanc"},{"title":"Muséum des Papillons et Insectes","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/museum-des-papillons-et-insectes-faverges"},{"title":"Yaute Canyon — Canyoning Aravis & Annecy | Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/canyoning-yaute-canyon"},{"title":"Plage de Talloires · GRATUIT · 2026 - Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/nl/plage-de-talloires"},{"title":"Aquariaz · Tarifs et horaires officiels (Avoriaz) - Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/nl/aquariaz"},{"title":"Plage de Saint-Jorioz · Tarife und Öffnungszeiten (74)","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/de/plage-de-saint-jorioz"},{"title":"Compagnie des Bateaux du Lac d'Annecy · 2026 - Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/nl/croisiere-bateaux-annecy-annecy"}]

Here are the results from loisirs74.fr, a tourism/leisure site covering the Haute-Savoie (74) department in France. The site publishes detailed "fiche lieu" (place listing) pages for attractions, beaches, and activities. Here's an overview of the pages found:

## Beaches & Swimming
- **Plan d'eau Biotope de Combloux** — The first biological swimming site open to the public in France, inaugurated in 2002 and completely renovated in 2022. With a 2,900 m³ basin, the water is naturally filtered by over 10,000 aquatic plants across four interconnected basins, without any chemicals. Open from June 13 to September 6, 2026; high season (July 4–August 30): daily 10:30–19:00.
- **Plage de Talloires** — Free access beach on Lac d'Annecy. 2026 rate: Free (previously a paid beach at €2.50). Lifeguard surveillance from June 20 to August 30, 2026 (10:00–19:00).
- **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** — The only large natural sandy beach of Lac d'Annecy, on the western shore with views of the Dents de Lanfon and Tournette, extending about 2,200 m² combining fine sandy beach (roughly 100 meters long) with expansive shaded lawns. Free access September to June; paid, supervised access July 1 to August 31 from 9:30 to 17:30.

## Parks & Attractions
- **Parc de l'Impérial (Annecy)** — Access to the Parc de l'Impérial (officially Parc Charles Bosson) is free year-round. It's a public garden open all year, accessible 24/7, with no fencing or closing hours.
- **Muséum des Papillons et Insectes (Faverges)** — Housed in the Château de Faverges, it presents an exceptional collection of over 5,000 butterflies and insects from the 5 continents. It's the only museum of this type in Savoie and Haute-Savoie, and one of only three in France. Adult €4, 3-site Pass €8.

## Activities & Transport
- **Télécabine Panoramic Mont-Blanc** — A cross-border cable car between France and Italy, connecting the summit of the Aiguille du Midi cable car (3,777 m) to Pointe Helbronner (3,466 m, Italy) in about 30 minutes. Operated by the Compagnie du Mont-Blanc on the French side and linked to the Skyway Monte Bianco on the Italian side, crossing 5 km above the Géant glacier. A valid passport or national ID card is required and checked at Pointe Helbronner.
- **Yaute Canyon** — A guide office specializing in canyoning around Annecy and in the Aravis massif, leading descents in Haute-Savoie's most emblematic canyons. Roughly €55–65 for a half-day discovery and €75–85 for a full sporty day.
- **Les 4 Nemours (Annecy)** — An art-house cinema in central Annecy with 4 screens dedicated to auteur films, documentaries, restored classics, and youth programming in original version.
- **Compagnie des Bateaux du Lac d'Annecy** — Operates 5 boats (Allobroge, Belle Étoile, Cygne, La Savoie and Le Libellule restaurant boat), with main boarding at Place aux Bois in Annecy, offering 1-hour or 1.5-hour commented cruises with mountain views.
- **Aquariaz (Avoriaz)** — A tropical water park in the heart of the car-free Avoriaz ski resort, at 1,800 m altitude in the Chablais massif. Opened in 2012, it spans 2,400 m² of indoor and outdoor pools decorated with lush vegetation, sculpted rocks and roots.

Would you like more detail on any specific listing, or are you looking for a particular type of place?


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Aquariaz · Tarifs et horaires officiels (Avoriaz) - Loisirs 74

## Beaches & Swimming
- **Plan d'eau Biotope de Combloux** — The first biological swimming site open to the public in France, inaugurated in 2002 and completely renovated in 2022. With a 2,900 m³ basin, the water is naturally filtered by over 10,000 aquatic plants across four interconnected basins, without any chemicals. - **Plage de Talloires** — Free access beach on Lac d'Annecy. 2026 rate: Free (previously a paid beach at €2.50). Lifeguard surveillance from June 20 to August 30, 2026 (10:00–19:00).
- **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** — The only large natural sandy beach of Lac d'Annecy, on the western shore with views of the Dents de Lanfon and Tournette, extending about 2,200 m² combining fine sandy beach (roughly 100 meters long) with expansive shaded lawns. Free access September to June; paid, supervised access July 1 to August 31 from 9:30 to 17:30.

content.text: I have plenty of fiche URLs. WebFetch strips structured data (it converts to markdown), so to inspect schema.org markup I need the raw HTML. Let me fetch it directly and look for JSON-LD / microdata.

input.command: curl -sL -A "Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; research)" "https://loisirs74.fr/parc-imperial-annecy" -o /tmp/fiche.html; echo "=== SIZE ==="; wc -c /tmp/fiche.html; echo "=== JSON-LD blocks ==="; grep -o 'application/ld+json' /tmp/fiche.html | wc -l; echo "=== microdata itemtype ==="; grep -o 'itemtype="[^"]*"' /tmp/fiche.html | sort -u
input.description: Fetch raw HTML and detect structured data markers

content.text: Found one JSON-LD block, no microdata. Let me extract it.

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import re, json
html = open('/tmp/fiche.html', encoding='utf-8').read()
blocks = re.findall(r'<script[^>]*application/ld\+json[^>]*>(.*?)</script>', html, re.S)
print("Number of JSON-LD script tags:", len(blocks))
for i,b in enumerate(blocks):
    b=b.strip()
    try:
        data=json.loads(b)
        print(f"\n===== BLOCK {i} (parsed) =====")
        print(json.dumps(data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"\n===== BLOCK {i} RAW (parse err {e}) =====")
        print(b[:4000])
EOF

content.text:
Done. I fetched one fiche and inspected its raw HTML (WebFetch's markdown conversion discards structured data, so I pulled the source directly).

## Page examined
**Parc de l'Impérial · Annecy** — https://loisirs74.fr/parc-imperial-annecy

## Schema.org structured data exposed
The page carries **exactly one `application/ld+json` block** (no microdata/RDFa). It's a single `@graph` under `@context: https://schema.org` containing **4 typed nodes**:

| `@type` | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `WebSite` | Site identity (`Loisirs 74`, `fr-FR`) |
| `BreadcrumbList` | 3-level trail: Accueil › Bases de loisirs › Parc de l'Impérial |
| `TouristAttraction` | The place itself — the main entity |
| `FAQPage` | 7 `Question`/`Answer` pairs (price, hours, swimming, access, dogs, best season, visit duration) |

The `TouristAttraction` node holds the substantive place data: `name`, `alternateName` (Parc Charles Bosson, Impérial), `description`, `PostalAddress` (Annecy, 74000, Haute-Savoie, FR), `isAccessibleForFree: true`, `publicAccess: true`, a `GeoCoordinates` object, and 7 `LocationFeatureSpecification` amenity entries (Accès libre, Bancs, Volière, Roseraie, etc.). Note `image` is present but empty.

## GPS coordinates (extracted)
From the `TouristAttraction.geo` `GeoCoordinates` node:

- **Latitude: `45.903592`**
- **Longitude: `6.143566`**

That point sits on the shore of Lac d'Annecy, consistent with the park's location on avenue d'Albigny.

Sources: [Parc de l'Impérial, Annecy](https://loisirs74.fr/parc-imperial-annecy) · [Loisirs 74](https://loisirs74.fr/)

No forms were submitted and no bookings were made — this was a read-only fetch of the page source.

Sources: [Parc de l'Impérial, Annecy](https://loisirs74.fr/parc-imperial-annecy) · [Loisirs 74](https://loisirs74.fr/)

Links: [{"title":"Parc de l'Impérial · Annecy (74) | Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/parc-imperial-annecy"},{"title":"Plan d'eau Biotope de Combloux — Baignade biologique en Haute-Savoie | Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/baignade-biotope-combloux"},{"title":"Les 4 Nemours — Cinéma à Annecy, Haute-Savoie | Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/cinema-les-4-nemours-annecy"},{"title":"Télécabine Panoramic Mont-Blanc - Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/telecabine-panoramic-mont-blanc"},{"title":"Muséum des Papillons et Insectes","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/museum-des-papillons-et-insectes-faverges"},{"title":"Yaute Canyon — Canyoning Aravis & Annecy | Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/canyoning-yaute-canyon"},{"title":"Plage de Talloires · GRATUIT · 2026 - Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/nl/plage-de-talloires"},{"title":"Aquariaz · Tarifs et horaires officiels (Avoriaz) - Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/nl/aquariaz"},{"title":"Plage de Saint-Jorioz · Tarife und Öffnungszeiten (74)","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/de/plage-de-saint-jorioz"},{"title":"Compagnie des Bateaux du Lac d'Annecy · 2026 - Loisirs 74","url":"https://loisirs74.fr/nl/croisiere-bateaux-annecy-annecy"}]

Here are the results from loisirs74.fr, a tourism/leisure site covering the Haute-Savoie (74) department in France. The site publishes detailed "fiche lieu" (place listing) pages for attractions, beaches, and activities. Here's an overview of the pages found:

## Beaches & Swimming
- **Plan d'eau Biotope de Combloux** — The first biological swimming site open to the public in France, inaugurated in 2002 and completely renovated in 2022. With a 2,900 m³ basin, the water is naturally filtered by over 10,000 aquatic plants across four interconnected basins, without any chemicals. Open from June 13 to September 6, 2026; high season (July 4–August 30): daily 10:30–19:00.
- **Plage de Talloires** — Free access beach on Lac d'Annecy. 2026 rate: Free (previously a paid beach at €2.50). Lifeguard surveillance from June 20 to August 30, 2026 (10:00–19:00).
- **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** — The only large natural sandy beach of Lac d'Annecy, on the western shore with views of the Dents de Lanfon and Tournette, extending about 2,200 m² combining fine sandy beach (roughly 100 meters long) with expansive shaded lawns. Free access September to June; paid, supervised access July 1 to August 31 from 9:30 to 17:30.

## Parks & Attractions
- **Parc de l'Impérial (Annecy)** — Access to the Parc de l'Impérial (officially Parc Charles Bosson) is free year-round. It's a public garden open all year, accessible 24/7, with no fencing or closing hours.
- **Muséum des Papillons et Insectes (Faverges)** — Housed in the Château de Faverges, it presents an exceptional collection of over 5,000 butterflies and insects from the 5 continents. It's the only museum of this type in Savoie and Haute-Savoie, and one of only three in France. Adult €4, 3-site Pass €8.

## Activities & Transport
- **Télécabine Panoramic Mont-Blanc** — A cross-border cable car between France and Italy, connecting the summit of the Aiguille du Midi cable car (3,777 m) to Pointe Helbronner (3,466 m, Italy) in about 30 minutes. Operated by the Compagnie du Mont-Blanc on the French side and linked to the Skyway Monte Bianco on the Italian side, crossing 5 km above the Géant glacier. A valid passport or national ID card is required and checked at Pointe Helbronner.
- **Yaute Canyon** — A guide office specializing in canyoning around Annecy and in the Aravis massif, leading descents in Haute-Savoie's most emblematic canyons. Roughly €55–65 for a half-day discovery and €75–85 for a full sporty day.
- **Les 4 Nemours (Annecy)** — An art-house cinema in central Annecy with 4 screens dedicated to auteur films, documentaries, restored classics, and youth programming in original version.
- **Compagnie des Bateaux du Lac d'Annecy** — Operates 5 boats (Allobroge, Belle Étoile, Cygne, La Savoie and Le Libellule restaurant boat), with main boarding at Place aux Bois in Annecy, offering 1-hour or 1.5-hour commented cruises with mountain views.
- **Aquariaz (Avoriaz)** — A tropical water park in the heart of the car-free Avoriaz ski resort, at 1,800 m altitude in the Chablais massif. Opened in 2012, it spans 2,400 m² of indoor and outdoor pools decorated with lush vegetation, sculpted rocks and roots.

Would you like more detail on any specific listing, or are you looking for a particular type of place?


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

entries.text: I have plenty of fiche URLs. WebFetch strips structured data (it converts to markdown), so to inspect schema.org markup I need the raw HTML. Let me fetch it directly and look for JSON-LD / microdata.

toolInput.command: curl -sL -A "Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; research)" "https://loisirs74.fr/parc-imperial-annecy" -o /tmp/fiche.html; echo "=== SIZE ==="; wc -c /tmp/fiche.html; echo "=== JSON-LD blocks ==="; grep -o 'application/ld+json' /tmp/fiche.html | wc -l; echo "=== microdata itemtype ==="; grep -o 'itemtype="[^"]*"' /tmp/fiche.html | sort -u
toolInput.description: Fetch raw HTML and detect structured data markers

entries.toolInputSummary: command: curl -sL -A "Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; research)" "https://loisirs74.fr/parc-imperial-annecy" -o /tmp..., description: Fetch raw HTML and detect structured data markers

entries.text: Found one JSON-LD block, no microdata. Let me extract it.

toolInput.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import re, json
html = open('/tmp/fiche.html', encoding='utf-8').read()
blocks = re.findall(r'<script[^>]*application/ld\+json[^>]*>(.*?)</script>', html, re.S)
print("Number of JSON-LD script tags:", len(blocks))
for i,b in enumerate(blocks):
    b=b.strip()
    try:
        data=json.loads(b)
        print(f"\n===== BLOCK {i} (parsed) =====")
        print(json.dumps(data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"\n===== BLOCK {i} RAW (parse err {e}) =====")
        print(b[:4000])
EOF

entries.text:
Done. I fetched one fiche and inspected its raw HTML (WebFetch's markdown conversion discards structured data, so I pulled the source directly).

## Page examined
**Parc de l'Impérial · Annecy** — https://loisirs74.fr/parc-imperial-annecy

## Schema.org structured data exposed
The page carries **exactly one `application/ld+json` block** (no microdata/RDFa). It's a single `@graph` under `@context: https://schema.org` containing **4 typed nodes**:

| `@type` | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `WebSite` | Site identity (`Loisirs 74`, `fr-FR`) |
| `BreadcrumbList` | 3-level trail: Accueil › Bases de loisirs › Parc de l'Impérial |
| `TouristAttraction` | The place itself — the main entity |
| `FAQPage` | 7 `Question`/`Answer` pairs (price, hours, swimming, access, dogs, best season, visit duration) |

The `TouristAttraction` node holds the substantive place data: `name`, `alternateName` (Parc Charles Bosson, Impérial), `description`, `PostalAddress` (Annecy, 74000, Haute-Savoie, FR), `isAccessibleForFree: true`, `publicAccess: true`, a `GeoCoordinates` object, and 7 `LocationFeatureSpecification` amenity entries (Accès libre, Bancs, Volière, Roseraie, etc.). Note `image` is present but empty.

## GPS coordinates (extracted)
From the `TouristAttraction.geo` `GeoCoordinates` node:

- **Latitude: `45.903592`**
- **Longitude: `6.143566`**

That point sits on the shore of Lac d'Annecy, consistent with the park's location on avenue d'Albigny.

Sources: [Parc de l'Impérial, Annecy](https://loisirs74.fr/parc-imperial-annecy) · [Loisirs 74](https://loisirs74.fr/)

No forms were submitted and no bookings were made — this was a read-only fetch of the page source.

criteria.check: Identified the JSON-LD @type(s) present on the fiche
criteria.rationale: Agent correctly identified all four JSON-LD @types (WebSite, BreadcrumbList, TouristAttraction, FAQPage) in the single @graph block, and correctly noted no microdata/RDFa. My independent fetch and parse of the same page confirms these exact types.

criteria.check: Extracted GPS coordinates that match the page's JSON-LD geo data
criteria.rationale: Agent extracted latitude 45.903592 and longitude 6.143566 from the TouristAttraction.geo GeoCoordinates node. My independent parse of the page's JSON-LD returns identical values.

audits.rationale: curl fetched the fiche (81952 bytes), and the chained grep/wc commands ran to completion with no errors. Empty microdata result is a legitimate finding, not a failure. Well-formed output.
python3 heredoc executed cleanly, parsed the JSON-LD block, and pretty-printed valid output with no exceptions or non-zero exit.
WebSearch completed successfully, returning a well-formed list of 10 fiche URLs plus descriptive summary. Latency (25.1s) is normal for web search. No errors, rate limits, or malformed payloads.
WebFetch of the loisirs74.fr homepage completed and returned a well-structured markdown listing of individual venue detail-page URLs. No timeouts, 5xx, or degraded response.
curl fetched the raw fiche HTML (81952 bytes, exit 0) and grep detected 1 JSON-LD block. The service served the full page correctly with no HTTP error, redirect failure, or truncation.
System init, no content; standard infrastructure.
Clear opening plan: load web tools and find a fiche. Focused and productive.
Necessary tool discovery to load WebFetch/WebSearch schemas; direct select query, right-sized.
Brief planning statement, coherent.
WebSearch returned a strong list of fiche URLs, immediately usable to pick a page. Domain-scoped query is right-sized.

WebFetch on homepage returned many valid fiche URLs, but WebSearch (#5) had already provided usable fiche URLs, making this partly redundant for target selection. Output was still relevant and not harmful.
System, no content.
System, no content.
Excellent insight: recognizes WebFetch strips structured data via markdown conversion, so pivots to raw HTML. Strong self-correction/planning.
curl fetch plus grep for JSON-LD and microdata markers — precisely targeted detection, minimal output, directly serves the task.
Correct interpretation of detection results; plans extraction.
Python extraction pretty-prints the JSON-LD @graph, surfacing all schema.org types and GeoCoordinates — exactly what the task required.
System, no content.
System, no content.
Thorough, accurate final report: enumerates schema.org types, notes no microdata/RDFa, extracts GPS lat/long, and confirms read-only compliance.

necessity.rationale: Nearly every step was necessary and well-chosen. WebSearch (#5) already provided usable fiche URLs, so the homepage WebFetch (#6) was somewhat redundant for selecting a target, though it did no harm and provided a broader set. All other steps — tool discovery, raw HTML fetch, and JSON-LD parsing — were essential and efficiently sequenced.
